summary: First-iteration full-stack test of Ascent CRM. 32/32 backend pytest cases pass after fixing one real ObjectId-leak bug in the audit() helper. Frontend critical flows verified: login (Midnight Mountain dark theme rgb(11,15,21)), dashboard, pipeline kanban (7 stages with altitude badges Basecamp/Ascent/Summit/Closed Won/Closed Lost), AI Studio (Gemini 3 generates 688-char draft with Fields-Used panel showing brand_voice + products + incoming_email grounding entities), and public lead form /f/discovery loads without auth. All 11 module routes load without error. Stripe checkout returns a real https session URL. Quote->Invoice flow computes totals correctly and produces INV-YYYY-NNNN numbering.

critical.issue: audit() stored Motor-mutated docs which contained nested BSON ObjectId in `after`, making GET /api/audit return HTTP 500 (jsonable_encoder fails). Defensive fix applied (recursive _strip_oid in audit()) and 5 corrupted DB rows cleaned. Root cause: _insert() passes `doc` to audit() AFTER insert_one() injected `_id`. Recommend also stripping _id at the end of _insert() return path or doing copy-before-insert.

minor.issue: Accepts submissions with consent_given=false (returns 200). Consent should be required before storing PII per GDPR. Currently no validation — risk for privacy compliance.
Email is silently lowercased — fine, but not documented; consider returning 400 on uppercase to make intent explicit, or document behaviour.

ui_bugs.component: Dashboard 'Altitude Map / Pipeline by stage' + Pipeline Kanban column counts
ui_bugs.issue: All 7 stage rows show count 0 and $0.00 even though 'Open Pipeline' KPI shows $116,400.00 / 4 deals open. Kanban columns also show 0 deal cards. Likely the frontend groups deals by `stage_id` while backend stores `pipeline_stage_id` — field-name mismatch. Verify the Pipeline.jsx + Dashboard.jsx aggregation key matches Deal model field `pipeline_stage_id`.

issues: React hydration warning: a <span> is being rendered as a child of <option>. Affects accessibility/console hygiene; check Pipeline.jsx deal-stage <option> children that include altitude badge spans.

action_items: Fix Dashboard altitude-map and Pipeline kanban grouping to use Deal.pipeline_stage_id (not stage_id) so cards appear in their columns.
Strip <span> children from inside <option> elements (deal-stage <select>) to silence React hydration warning.
Enforce consent_given=true on /api/forms/{slug}/submit (return 400 otherwise) for GDPR compliance.
Optionally harden _insert() to copy doc before insert_one to keep callers from receiving _id-mutated dicts.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1384 lines — recommend splitting into routers/ (auth, crm, billing, ai, gdpr) to stay <700 LOC per file.

_insert() returns the Motor-mutated dict (with _id) to callers. Currently FastAPI's response model strips it, but the same dict is also passed to audit(), where it leaked as nested ObjectId — fixed in audit() but the root pattern is fragile.
Ensure every mutating endpoint that writes audit copies/serialises payload before persistence to avoid future ObjectId/datetime serialisation bugs.
seed_data_creation: Used auto-seeded demo data only. Created TEST_-prefixed companies/contacts during CRUD tests. AI generations and audit entries also accumulated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend audit ObjectId bug already fixed; if main agent rewrites _insert to copy doc, re-run pytest to confirm. Frontend grouping bug is in Pipeline.jsx + Dashboard.jsx — they likely aggregate deals by `d.stage_id` but Deal model now uses `d.pipeline_stage_id`. After fix, verify kanban shows the seeded deals in their columns and the Altitude Map widget shows non-zero counts.
rca of the issue: TestAudit/list_audit returned 500 because Motor's insert_one mutates the supplied dict to attach `_id` (BSON ObjectId). _insert() then passes that mutated dict to audit() which stores `after`={..., _id: ObjectId(...)}. GET /api/audit projects out the top-level _id, but nested ObjectId inside `after` still trips fastapi.encoders.jsonable_encoder. Mitigation: recursive _strip_oid before insertion + one-time DB cleanup of the 5 already-poisoned rows.
